channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal agents fear for their families amid tensions, violence
item.description: An ICE officer told NewsNation he and colleagues fear they're facing real danger in the current social climate.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Maine Gov. Janet Mills will challenge Sen. Susan Collins in key 2026 Senate contest, AP sources say
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Maine’s two-term Democratic Gov. Janet Mills will run for the U.S. Senate seat held by veteran Republican Sen. Susan Collins next year, two people familiar with Mills’ plans said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: The Trump administration has provided a $300 million infusion to a food aid program for low-income mothers and children, easing concerns about funding during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump officials not giving up on deporting Abrego Garcia to African nations despite resistance
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — The Trump administration is pushing again to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was mistakenly sent to El Salvador, even though three African countries they’ve approached have rejected the idea, a judge heard during testimony Friday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Biden-era 'unvetted' immigrants nabbed after Virginia highway gunfire as border debate hits governor's race
item.description: Two illegal immigrant teens from Honduras face deportation detainers after allegedly shooting from a vehicle on a busy Interstate 295 east of Richmond, Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Symphony Orchestra to open all of its Kennedy Center shows with the national anthem
item.description: Kennedy Center partners with Vet Tix to donate more than $100,000 in tickets to veterans, while the National Symphony Orchestra embraces patriotic programming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MIT president says she 'cannot support' proposal to adopt Trump priorities for funding benefits
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The president of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology said Friday she “cannot support” a White House proposal that asks MIT and eight other universities to adopt President Donald Trump's political agenda in exchange for favorable access to federal funding.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Worries grow over air traffic staffing as shutdown drags on
item.description: Air travel is expected to rise in the United States during the three-day Columbus Day holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: Marcus Espinoza, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: Republican Jensen makes another try for Minnesota governor
item.description: MPR News politics editor talked with Republican candidate Scott Jensen about his second bid for governor. Then, a look at how farmers are coping with trade barriers as harvest time is here.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. links autism with Tylenol given during circumcision
item.description: The Trump administration has promoted the theory that a rise in autism cases is the result of external factors.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump says 8 kids displaced by Russia-Ukraine war reunite with families after Putin talks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Melania Trump announced Friday that eight children displaced by the Russia-Ukraine war have been reunited with their families following ongoing talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Melania Trump set to make announcement
item.description: First lady Melania Trump gave an announcement Friday morning from the White House. Her remarks came after Amazon announced Thursday that its "Melania" documentary will be released in theaters on Jan. 30, 2026, and then later be available on its Prime Video platform.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Budget office says ‘substantial’ firings of federal workers have started
item.description: The White House budget office said Friday that mass firings of federal workers have started in an attempt to exert more pressure on Democratic lawmakers as the government shutdown continues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Social Security workers say the shutdown has them unable to help with benefit letters
item.description: Employees at Social Security field offices say the government shutdown has left them unable to carry out an important service: help recipients with benefit verification letters.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez, MPR News

item.title: Trump gets long-sought Gaza hostage deal with a whole lot of help from Arab and Muslim allies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After months of gridlock, President Donald Trump finally landed a long-sought Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage deal in Gaza — an agreement that only came together after a weekslong diplomacy blitz and a whole lot of help from some Arab and Muslim allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, AAMER MADHANI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Senate unanimously endorses repeal of 2002 Iraq war resolution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than two decades later, Congress is on the verge of writing a closing chapter to the war in Iraq. The Senate voted Thursday to repeal the resolution that authorized the 2003 U.S. invasion, following a House vote last month that would return the basic war power to Congress.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The Senate approved its massive annual defense policy bill late Thursday as the U.S. government remains shut down. The GOP-led chamber approved the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) 70-20, aiming to fund the U.S. military at $924.7 billion in fiscal 2026.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, NewsNation

item.title: US is sending about 200 troops to Israel to help support and monitor the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is sending about 200 troops to Israel to help support and monitor the ceasefire deal in Gaza as part of a team that includes partner nations, nongovernmental organizations and private-sector players, U.S. officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government shutdown threatens funding for SNAP and WIC in New York
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX 11) – The government shutdown reached its ninth day on Thursday, as the Senate again failed to find a way forward on funding the government for the next fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11

item.title: Judge blocks National Guard deployment in Illinois for 2 weeks
item.description: A judge has blocked the deployment of National Guard troops in the Chicago area for two weeks, finding no substantial evidence that a “danger of rebellion” is brewing in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida bill proposes $1,000 yearly property tax rebate
item.description: The debate around property taxes is already leading most conversations at the Florida State Capitol before next session has even begun, and with many options on the table, one of them looks to send $1,000 a year to certain homesteaded property owners here in the state.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York Attorney General Letitia James indicted
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A grand jury has indicted New York Attorney General Letitia James on a fraud charge, a person familiar with the matter told The Associated Press on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Oregon general's comments raise eyebrows a week later
item.description: In the clip, Brigadier General Alan Gronewold said Oregon National Guard members “will be protecting any protesters at the ICE facility.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina Rep. Cecil Brockman accused of statutory rape with 15-year-old victim, warrants allege; Medical emergency delays court appearance
item.description: GUILFORD COUNTY, N.C. (WGHP) -- A North Carolina State House of Representatives member who represents Guilford County has been arrested and charged with multiple sex crimes.
item.dc:creator: Emily Mikkelsen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump Cabinet meeting focuses on shutdown, crime
item.description: The president is said to be considering invoking the Insurrection Act as he sends National Guard troops to Portland and Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s use of National Guard strays from role as ‘minutemen,’ military experts say
item.description: Military experts say they also worry how these new deployments will affect recruitment and public trust.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Democrats win momentum over GOP in shutdown fight
item.description: As the shutdown fight stretches into its second week, the winds have shifted in favor of the Democrats — at least for the moment. Early polls say voters are more likely to blame President Trump and the Republicans for the lengthy impasse.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Gov. Lombardo stresses 'desperate need' for stronger DUI laws after 12-year-old's death
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo is once again calling for stricter DUI penalties -- this time following the death of a 12-year-old boy. Oh’Ryan Brooks, 27, faces DUI and reckless driving-related charges after the deadly crash on Oct. 3.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How the government shutdown is affecting air travel
item.description: A dozen facilities saw air traffic control shortages on Monday, delaying flights at several airports. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy blamed “a slight tick-up in sick calls” due to the shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman and Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court seems skeptical about state bans on 'conversion therapy' for LBGTQ+ kids
item.description: A majority of Supreme Court justices seem to be leaning in favor of a Christian counselor who is challenging bans on LGBTQ+ “conversion therapy” for kids as a violation of her First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minn. Farm Bureau president on soybean bailout, tariffs
item.description: Soybeans make up for a quarter of Minnesota’s exports, which equates to about $2 billion in annual sales — and China has yet to buy a single bean. Minnesota Farm Bureau President Dan Glessing joined Morning Edition Monday to talk about trade, tariffs and ripple effects of the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Early voting begins for November special election
item.description: Early voting for the Nov. 4 special election, which includes the highly contested Proposition 50, kicked off in California on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: National Guard troops report to Illinois as lawsuit against Trump administration seeks to halt their deployment
item.description: CHICAGO (AP/WGN) — Hundreds of Illinois National Guard troops reported for duty in the Chicago area Tuesday morning, and they'll be joined by members of the Texas National Guard for what the Trump administration is calling a "federal protective mission.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
